title: peucedanum elatior
intro: Peucedanum elatior, also known as tall hog's fennel, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include its carminative, diuretic, and anti-inflammatory effects. Peucedanum elatior has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, urinary problems, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve spasms. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
